# CHOIVE

> CHOIVE shows whether Claude, ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Gemini recommend a business or another business. It reports each platform separately, explains the evidence behind the result, and shows what the business can improve.

## What CHOIVE is

CHOIVE is a point-in-time AI recommendation diagnostic for businesses. A user enters a business name, website, category, and location. CHOIVE checks public website and search evidence, runs separately attributed recommendation measurements, identifies relevant competitors, and returns the CHOIVE Index out of 100.

Website: https://choive.com/
Methodology: https://choive.com/methodology
Free diagnostic: https://choive.com/#diagnostic
Report: https://choive.com/report
Contact: hello@choive.com

## What the diagnostic measures

CHOIVE measures four evidence-based pillars:

1. Clarity: how clearly the business explains what it does, for whom, and where.
2. Trust: whether independent public evidence supports the business's credibility and claims.
3. Difference: whether the business has a specific, evidenced reason to be chosen over alternatives.
4. Ease: whether people, search engines, and AI systems can find, read, and act on its information.

Each pillar is worth 25 points. Together they form the CHOIVE Index out of 100.

The pillar scores are calculated from evidence about the subject business. AI recommendation transcripts, competitor identities, and whether a provider recommended the subject do not automatically raise or lower the pillar scores.

The current score method is evidence-rubric-v2. Code allocates numeric points from recorded evidence statuses and stores a point ledger with the observed evidence, verification type, and source where available. A model cannot submit its own final score. Interpreted checks are labelled model assessed and receive lower confidence than mechanical or independently retrieved evidence.

## AI platform measurements

CHOIVE reports four provider lanes separately:

- Claude: measured through the Anthropic API.
- ChatGPT: measured through the OpenAI API.
- Perplexity: measured through the Perplexity API.
- Gemini: measured through the Google Gemini API.

Matching company names are not merged. Each lane states what that provider returned during the diagnostic run.

An API response is a real provider response for that run, but it is not guaranteed to match every consumer-app session. Consumer apps may use different models, search context, product settings, conversation history, location, or personalization.

CHOIVE records one response for each unbranded visibility question. The branded replacement question normally records three independent responses. A provider-level recommendation requires more than half of the completed responses to name the same company. Failed responses remain failures and are not converted into negative recommendations.

## Competitor definitions

- AI recommendation: a company named by a specific provider in the recorded answer.
- Head-to-head competitor: the closest verified purchasing substitute serving the same buyer type with a comparable product or service in a market the subject can serve.
- Wider market competitor: a relevant company in the broader category that is not necessarily the closest substitute.

CHOIVE does not label a market competitor as an AI recommendation unless the recorded provider response names it.

## Products and prices

- CHOIVE Diagnostic: free. Headline verdict, CHOIVE Index, four pillar scores, provider recommendations when successfully measured, and competitor exposure.
- CHOIVE Analysis: $99 one-time. Full online evidence breakdown, platform coverage, competitor comparison, prioritized actions, and ready-to-use assets.
- CHOIVE Report: $499 one-time. A structured ten-section report with the Analysis findings, implementation milestones, verification criteria, and a 30-day plan. Future scores are not predicted; progress is measured by a later diagnostic.

There are no recurring subscriptions for these three products.

## How CHOIVE differs from monitoring platforms

AI visibility monitoring platforms track mentions, citations, and share of voice over time. CHOIVE runs a focused diagnostic: it records separately attributed provider recommendations, evaluates the subject business across four public-evidence pillars, distinguishes AI recommendations from market competitors, and provides prioritized improvements.

CHOIVE does not claim to be a replacement for every monitoring platform. Continuous enterprise monitoring and a point-in-time diagnostic solve different needs.

## Accuracy and limitations

- Every new diagnostic should perform new provider and public-evidence measurements.
- Earlier provider answers are not reused as the answer to a new run.
- Provider failures or uncertain names should be shown as unavailable, partial, or not established rather than guessed.
- Results are dated snapshots and may change between runs.
- CHOIVE cannot guarantee future AI recommendations, consumer-app parity, future scores, or commercial outcomes.

Full methodology and limitations: https://choive.com/methodology
